index.description: Restore a broken thing to working state. Output is service / build / pipeline back to green. Production incidents, broken builds, failed deploys, security breaches, account lockouts, escalations.

playbook.summary: Agent ran 40 turns and lost the plot → reconstruct trajectory, find bad transition, ship a guardrail.
An agent ran 40 turns and lost the plot. Reconstruct the trajectory, find the bad state transition, ship a guardrail.
Agent shipped hallucinated answer / mis-called tool / jailbroke itself → blameless postmortem attributes cause to layer, lands regression eval, prevents recurrence.
Defined sequence to diagnose, stabilize, and restore confidence on a red-flagged project before contract loss.
Take over or stabilise a project that has missed two consecutive milestones, lost client trust, or has red on cost/schedule/quality. Exit with a credible recovery plan executing for 3+ weeks.
Customer-reported fabricated answer → reproduce, attribute, ship fix or guardrail, log to permanent eval-set within the working day.
Paging alert → contained impact → human-published postmortem → merged preventive PRs in backlog.
"Clean slate: deps reinstalled, services running, seed data loaded, all tests pass, can develop without fighting the toolchain. Documented in README so a teammate can do it in <30 min."

"Done = an architecture escalation during an incident is resolved without short-circuiting the IC: architect provides options + trade-offs in writing, IC chooses, decision is logged and links into t..."

When the agent ships a hallucinated answer, mis-calls a tool, or jailbreaks itself, the team produces a structured postmortem that attributes cause to model/prompt/tool/context layer, lands a regre...
Alert fires → on-call triages within minutes → every alert closes with a verdict (noise / known / new incident) + audit log.

"An alert fires in production. On-call engineer triages within minutes using AI-classified context, decides: noise / known / new incident. New incidents enter a runbook flow; known issues get cross-..."
Production alert is acknowledged, reproduced locally, root-caused, fixed with a regression test, deployed, and the alert is either resolved or explicitly suppressed with a linked ticket.
Alert at 3am → root-caused, mitigated, postmortem-documented incident for one person without SRE rotation.
From alert ping at 3am to root-caused, mitigated, postmortem-documented incident — designed for one person, not a SRE rotation.
